edges.description: Reduced extracellular adenosine signaling increases tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase activity in patient cells.
edges.source: NT5E/CD73 loss impairs extracellular adenosine generation

edges.description: Increased TNAP activity reduces pyrophosphate-mediated inhibition of mineral deposition.
Hydroxyapatite deposition in lower-extremity arteries manifests as arterial calcification.
Calcific lower-extremity arterial disease causes exertional limb ischemia and claudication.
Hydroxyapatite deposition around joints manifests as periarticular calcification.
Periarticular crystal deposition causes chronic arthritis-like joint inflammation and symptoms.
Progressive periarticular calcification produces severe joint pain and deformity.
CD73-deficient vascular remodeling includes tortuosity and arteriomegaly alongside calcification.

nodes.description: Lower-extremity arterial calcification is a defining vascular manifestation.

evidence.snippet: We identified nine persons with calcifications of the lower-extremity arteries and hand and foot joint capsules: all five siblings in one family, three siblings in another, and one patient in a third family.

nodes.description: Arterial tortuosity and arteriomegaly are distinctive vascular remodeling features reported in CD73 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Vascular Disease in Mice and Humans Deficient in CD73
evidence.snippet: CD73-deficient humans present with the complex phenotype of vascular calcification, arteriomegaly and tortuosity, and calcification in small joints.

nodes.description: Severe joint pain and deformities are major burdens of disease.

evidence.reference_title: Pilot study to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of etidronate treatment for arterial calcification due to deficiency of CD73 (ACDC)
evidence.snippet: Arterial calcification due to deficiency of CD73 (ACDC; OMIM 211800) is a rare genetic disease resulting in calcium deposits in arteries and small joints causing claudication, resting pain, severe joint pain, and deformities.

nodes.description: Patients can present to rheumatology with chronic or recurrent arthritis-like symptoms before the genetic diagnosis is recognized.

evidence.reference_title: Calcification of Joints and Arteries (CALJA) Is a Rare Cause of Arthritis and Lower Limb Ischemia: Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Calcification of Joints and Arteries (CALJA) is a rare disease that leads to chronic arthritis and lower limb claudication due to hydroxyapatite crystal deposition.

nodes.description: Colchicine has case-report evidence for improving inflammatory symptoms and inflammatory markers in CALJA/HAAMC, but it is not established as disease-modifying therapy for calcification.

evidence.reference_title: Calcification of Joints and Arteries (CALJA) Is a Rare Cause of Arthritis and Lower Limb Ischemia: Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Our case is the first in which clinical symptoms and a steady increase of inflammatory markers improved only after colchicine therapy initiation.

nodes.description: CD73 deficiency increases alkaline phosphatase activity and reduces the anti-mineralization balance of pyrophosphate-related pathways, promoting calcium phosphate or hydroxyapatite deposition in lower-extremity arteries and periarticular tissues.

evidence.snippet: adenosine treatment reduced the levels of alkaline phosphatase and calcification.

evidence.reference_title: Calcification of Joints and Arteries (CALJA) Is a Rare Cause of Arthritis and Lower Limb Ischemia: Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The clinical features of CALJA are caused by hydroxyapatite crystal deposition at the periarticular and vascular levels due to abnormalities of pyrophosphate metabolism.

nodes.description: Etidronate has pilot human evidence for safety and possible slowing of lower-extremity vascular calcification progression, but it has not reversed established vascular or periarticular calcification.

evidence.reference_title: Pilot study to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of etidronate treatment for arterial calcification due to deficiency of CD73 (ACDC)
evidence.snippet: Etidronate treatment appeared to have slowed the progression of further vascular calcification in lower extremities as measured by CT but did not have an effect in reversing vascular and/or periarticular joint calcifications in our small ACDC cohort.

nodes.description: Lower-extremity arterial disease causes exertional limb pain and claudication.

evidence.snippet: All four siblings of Patient VI.1 had disabling intermittent claudication (ability to walk only 1 to 6 blocks) and hemodynamically significant lower-extremity obstructive peripheral artery disease

nodes.description: Biallelic NT5E variants produce nonfunctional CD73, reducing conversion of extracellular AMP to adenosine and disrupting an anti-calcification pathway in vascular and periarticular tissues.
nodes.id: NT5E/CD73 loss impairs extracellular adenosine generation

evidence.snippet: All mutations found in the three families result in nonfunctional CD73.
NT5E encodes CD73, a membrane-bound ecto-5′-nucleotidase (specifically, 5′–ribonucleotide phosphohydrolase; EC 3.1.3.5) involved in extracellular ATP metabolism.

nodes.description: Periarticular soft-tissue or joint-capsule calcification affects hands, feet, wrists, ankles, and other small joints.

evidence.reference_title: Imaging findings of arterial calcification due to deficiency of CD73: A case study
evidence.snippet: Dual energy CT studies of the extremities demonstrated extensive periarticular soft tissue calcification throughout the wrists, hands, ankle and feet without evidence of uric acid.

nodes.description: CD73-deficient patient fibroblasts show increased tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase activity, which degrades pyrophosphate and removes an anti-mineralization signal in vascular and periarticular tissues.

evidence.snippet: Cultured fibroblasts from affected members of Family 1 showed markedly reduced expression of NT5E messenger RNA, CD73 protein, and enzyme activity, as well as increased alkaline phosphatase levels and accumulated calcium phosphate crystals.
adenosine treatment reduced the levels of alkaline phosphatase and calcification.
